name: Macuilxochitl
description: Macuilxochitl, meaning 'Five Flower' in Nahuatl, was the Aztec god of games, dance, music, gambling, and pleasure. He was also considered the patron deity of nobles and the Ahuiateteo, the gods of excess. Often associated with Xochipilli, the 'Prince of Flowers', Macuilxochitl embodies the joyous and creative aspects of life. He was believed to bring good fortune in games of chance and inspire artistic expression. However, like many Aztec deities, he also had a darker side, being associated with excess and the potential for ruin that comes with unchecked indulgence. He was sometimes depicted with a painted face and adorned with flowers and feathers, reflecting his connection to beauty and celebration. Sacrifices, often of animals or symbolic offerings, were made to him to ensure continued prosperity and good luck. Macuilxochitl's influence permeated many aspects of Aztec society, from the elaborate ceremonies of the nobility to the everyday games played by commoners. His presence reminded the Aztecs of the importance of both celebrating life's joys and maintaining balance in their pursuits, lest pleasure turn into destruction.
subtitle: God of games, dance, and pleasure

keyMyths.title: The Origin of Macuilxochitl
keyMyths.description: Macuilxochitl is said to have sprung from the union of the primordial deities, representing the joy and beauty that emerges from creation. His birth heralded the beginning of artistry and playfulness in the Aztec world.

keyMyths.title: Macuilxochitl and Gambling
keyMyths.description: It was believed that Macuilxochitl influenced the outcome of games, favoring those who honored him with offerings. Gamblers would pray to him for good luck, seeking his divine intervention.

keyMyths.title: Macuilxochitl and the Ahuiateteo
keyMyths.description: As a patron of the Ahuiateteo, Macuilxochitl embodies the potential dangers of excess. He reminds people that even pleasure can lead to downfall if not tempered with moderation.

sources.author: Bernardino de Sahagún
sources.description: A comprehensive account of Aztec life and religion.
sources.text: A comprehensive account of Aztec life and religion.
